meaning: adj.严格的,严厉的,严酷的,严峻的
examples: The work failed to meet their rigorous standards.
Was it a curriculum that appeared more rigorous, perhaps?
All vitamin supplements would be evaluated in scientifically rigorous clinical trials.
Knowing where, when, and how to move will require rigorous market analysis with accurate data.
And he feels that change requires "rigorous performance measurement", the establishment of "concrete targets".
